tweets.text:
"There's a high level of interoperability between Robinhood chain and Lighter. A market maker can quickly move capital around between the two Lighter domains"

Vlad on how the Robinhood product differs from Lighter's own app

"For one thing, it has a unique front end access through Robinhood wallet. For many customers, if they already have the wallet app, they can use it seamlessly. If they have the Robinhood app, they can fund the wallet with USDG and start trading perps if they're in a jurisdiction that has it"

"It'll be a different mix of markets, settled in USDG stablecoin. We've already seen some of the markets folks are interested in trading are more stocks, some memes as well. So it'll be a different customer base, different mix of markets"

"One thing that's interesting on the back end is there's a high level of interoperability between Robinhood chain and Lighter. A market maker can quickly move capital around between the two Lighter domains. That creates a lot of advantages, because the market maker can participate on both in a much more efficient way than if they were trading on one exchange on Solana and another on Ethereum"

"All of these interop protocols, like a LayerZero, like a Chainlink CCIP, that's an oracle. They're just white labeled with a different name"

Niklas Kunkel, CEO of Chronicle Labs, on what people fail to recognize about bridges and interop

"Anytime you use cross chain communication, anytime you're bridging a token from one chain to another chain, these are all oracles, just white labeled with a different name, like cross chain or bridge"

"So it really comes down to the LayerZero oracle was compromised, and the DeFi protocol was insufficiently risk managed to deal with that"

"It's just going to be this ability to get leverage against your equities at much cheaper rates than you can in TradFi with your broker"

I have a bot running 24/7 buying and selling for 0 target profit just to waste IRS manpower and money.

I print and send the IRS all the documentation about my trades in paper form to stop them from automatizing the process with a computer program.

I mix blank pages, double print, ghost print or mark/write in them just in case they have a way to scan and use some sort of recognition software. That way they have to look at thousands upon thousands of pages one by one and look and random scribblings and drawn dicks while doing their job.

It's a whole pallet of papers with trades so I hire a guy to forklift that shit and send it just in the offchance that having to deal with a pallet is troublesome for them.

All the documents are numbered but I pack everything *completely out of order* (as in the lower numbers always happen to be at the bottom of the pallet) in case the order of the trades is important. If order is important they now have to deal lift boxes and have them laying around before they can even start checking the numbers.

My normal trades are also in those pages so they can't just ignore everything.

This costs me thousands of dollars every year. I make sure to send the invoices of how much all the processes cost me on top of the pallet so that whoever is in charge of it knows I'm wasting the equivalent of two months of his waging in asinine shit on top of his or her time.

Whatsapp, Signal and TG encrypted chats are fine. This law does not break encryption.

What it re-enables is warrantless scanning of unencrypted messages: Instagram, Discord, Snapchat, Gmail and even (unecrypted) iCloud.

Don't use those! Personally, Im moving to Proton

We must ensure encryption is not outlawed but seeing where the EU is going, they might try it.

Following so much FUD on X, I asked Claude Opus 4.8 for WHY this law was being pushed so much:

"Everyone assumes Chat Control is Big Tech wanting your data. Meta already has your data, it doesn't need a CSAM law to read your Instagram DMs, so that framing is emotionally satisfying and mostly wrong.

The actual money trail is narrower. Thorn, the anti-abuse "charity" that lobbies hardest for these laws, also sells the scanning software, including to US Homeland Security, which means it's lobbying to create demand for its own product. The Oak Foundation has put over $24m into pro-scanning advocacy since 2019, and Johansson's own directorate funded WeProtect while WeProtect campaigned for her regulation.

But the real driver isn't a company at all, it's the interior ministries. Law enforcement has wanted a lawful way into private and encrypted comms for twenty years, and CSAM is the perfect wedge because no politician can vote against "protect the children" without the headline writing itself. Build the scanning pipe for that and you've built general-purpose surveillance infra that gets pointed at terrorism next, then whatever's convenient after.

The tell is which measures get dropped when the encryption pushback lands. A centre to remove known material at the source, safety-by-design on apps, the stuff that protects kids without scanning everyone, keeps stalling. The mass-scanning part keeps getting forced through on procedural tricks. When the parts that help children without surveillance are the negotiable ones, that ordering tells you what the priority actually is."

tweets.text:
Aerodrome is changing how it pays for liquidity.

Emissions are paid in AERO while fees come in as dollars, so when AERO rises, the protocol gets less profitable if fees don't outpace the price. Every week it mints new tokens that are paid to liquidity providers, which is a real cost to the business.

Aerodrome aims to fix this with the AER Engine, which pegs emissions to revenue instead of the token price and caps inflation within a set band. Spend follows what the protocol earns rather than what the token does, so a rising AERO price no longer mechanically pushes earnings down.

It also changes the shape of the cost line. A fixed weekly mint held spending flat regardless of what the protocol brought in. Linking emissions to fees replaces that with a cost that scales with the business.

tweets.text:
The most overlooked corner of AI could be biology.

Capital spent the last three years chasing compute and robotics while AI for biology lagged behind. The 2021 biotech collapse hit longevity especially hard, and the sector never drew the enthusiasm the rest of AI did. That skepticism lingered even as record investment flowed into the space around it.

The technology has started to change that. The methods that cracked protein folding are now reaching cellular problems that were previously out of reach.

Companies like Altos Labs, NewLimit, and Life Biosciences have drawn major funding. The FDA doesn't recognize aging as a disease, so companies target specific age-related illnesses first. The real ambition is to treat aging itself.

10,000 Americans turn 65 every day, and Social Security's reserves are projected to deplete in the early 2030s. GLP-1s could be the start of a much larger longevity wave.

(Original content provided by @PonderingDurian at Delphi Ventures)

tweets.text:
Spending 10 years in TradFi & Crypto, I learned that
​
"Life is a Portfolio"
​
You allocate your time/conviction across assets the same way you allocate capital.
​
"The individual positions may underperform 90% of the time but the discipline to find that 10% is what compounds, turning your life around"
​
Each position serves a different purpose. Together they produce returns no single one could.
​
Investing in Onchain AI taught me to diversify into AI equities
​
But my journey in getting started with equities wasn’t really smooth (as expected)
​
> Cumbersome KYC process with new brokers
> Had to Offramp, moved capital to the bank, and wired it to the broker
> Need to move money around again if a broker doesn't have certain stock
> Tried 3 tokenized stocks platforms but faced 10-50% slippage, clunky UI/UX, & often times tradable on market hours only
​
Worst experience = when I swapped $2k into a quantum stock & ended up getting $1.1k
​
These challenges happened 6+ months ago, up until I found a platform (it recently surpassed $2B in cumulative vol) + another that made it possible for tokenized stocks to be used as collateral for perps (which makes delta-neutral strat, cross-equity leverage possible)
​
My Journey + Strategy + Thesis of Productive Stocks era below ↓
